title: Mid-execution ETH injections can fabricate the profitability check
reason: The code would indeed count any ETH that arrives before the final balance check, but this candidate is too speculative as a standalone issue because it depends on an in-transaction ETH injection from hardcoded external counterparties without source evidence they can do so; the practical balance-manipulation risk is already covered by F-002 and F-005.

title: Counter state is permissionlessly mutable by any address
reason: `Counter.sol` is a trivial unrestricted sample-style contract, and the report does not identify any privileged role, asset, invariant, or integration that would make public mutation a realistic security issue.
